results.claim: Overall score was 2.85, dropping to 2.03 without transcript quality component
Cumulative speedup only 1.053x but accuracy verification reduced effective shipped to 0 due to unverified lossy op
Campaign efficiency penalized: 3 of 4 implementation tracks failed (75% fail ratio), 4.0 penalty applied
Campaign status was campaign_exhausted after 3 rounds
bottleneck_analysis.md explicitly labeled fused_moe as having no pathological baseline at 79.6% BW, below the 85% threshold that justifies near-optimal classification
Round 1 debate summary adopted the near-optimal framing from bottleneck_analysis.md, treating it as definitive evidence that no fused_moe optimization path existed
Champion-1 projected 1.21% E2E improvement at BS=8 using cold-cache savings as projection basis
Official validated E2E: BS=8 actual speedup 0.99730 (NOISE verdict) vs projected 1.21% -- a >5x overestimate
8 total proposals with 100% grounding rate and 100% micro-experiment rate
100% micro-experiment rate across all proposals
Late finding partially substantiated: production topkGating is 4.6 us (not 8.46 us torch.topk used in prototype), correcting E2E from 1.49% to 0.41% for 3-op fusion
Filtering classified as incorrect, receiving only 0.5 points
Delegate confirmed DeepGEMM is blocked on L40S SM89 -- data not present in bottleneck_analysis.md
Champion-1 Round 2 cited delegate-1a findings with explicit [Source: delegate-1a] attribution
Delegate mapped all 4 per-layer w8a8 shapes including N=3072 and N=2560 -- shapes absent from bottleneck_analysis.md
Validation confirms 5 shapes tuned (including delegate-discovered N=3072 and N=2560), covering 120/160 calls
Phase 0 report established production config BLOCK_M=16, num_stages=3, grid=128 at BS=8
Round 2 report re-derives same production config, substantially overlapping Phase 0 findings
10 delegate invocations, 11 delegate artifacts, 66.7% citation rate across proposals
Counterfactual assessment: without delegate-3a, shipped optimization would have achieved <7% BS=1 improvement rather than 13.7%
E2E results validated: 13.7% BS=1, 5.3% BS=8, 0.7% BS=32 under production parity (CUDA graphs + torch.compile)
Champion vs validator kernel benchmarks agree within <1% for all shapes at BS=8 -- independent validation confirmed
Amdahl's Law ceiling analysis: f=1.26%, kernel speedup 1.146x, expected E2E=0.16% -- below 5-iteration noise floor
Two-run cross-comparison shows large measurement swings: BS=32 delta ranges from -41.5ms to +45.7ms, 2-run average speedups cluster near 1.0
WarpSpec is 3.4x slower than Triton across all batch sizes under CUDA graphs (production parity)
ncu diagnosis: 4.4x more instructions and 21% lower BW utilization due to FP8->FP16 conversion approach
op_fused_moe_route_prep FAILED: E2E 0.65% below 1.0% threshold despite correct 1.56x kernel speedup
Champion benchmarked outside CUDA graphs producing false positive 1.10x; actual production result was 0.29x
Production parity requires CUDA graphs -- the benchmark methodology that should have been used
BS=1 avg_latency was 0.185s -- impossible for 512 output tokens with 35B MoE model (correct is 3.177s)
Correct Stage 1 baseline values: BS=1: 3.177s, BS=8: 5.219s, BS=32: 10.470s -- 17x higher than the failed run
Bottleneck analysis identified w8a8 with default config but did not synthesize it into a candidate recommendation
Counterfactual: config tuning could have shipped in Round 1 instead of Round 2, saving ~3 hours across 6+ agents
Round 1 debate consumed 6 agents over 2 debate rounds, producing zero reusable output for the sole shipped optimization
Counterfactual: debate protocol should have diversified champions across top-5 components when dominant is near physical ceiling
FP8->FP16 conversion was required because the direct m16n8k32 FP8 MMA fragment layout on SM89 is undocumented
Both DeepGEMM and CUTLASS block FP8 are blocked on SM89 -- cross-model architectural constraint
Mechanism: replacing default BLOCK_SIZE_M=64 with BLOCK_SIZE_M=16 for M<=32 on L40S, reducing register pressure from 243 to ~100
Zero-code-change optimization with zero regression risk -- JSON config files only
After TP=4, intermediate_size_per_partition=128, creating extremely small GEMM shapes during decode
fused_moe_kernel dominates at 53.8% of non-NCCL compute at BS=8 -- typical for fine-grained 256-expert MoE
NCCL AllReduce spin-wait: 83-92% of total GPU time is spin-wait inflation, NOT true communication cost
NCCL AllReduce spin-wait absorbs 42-55% of any kernel-level savings
BS=1: 3.177s -> 2.741s, 1.159x speedup, 13.7% improvement, PASS
BS=8: 5.219s -> 4.940s, 1.056x speedup, 5.3% improvement, PASS
BS=32: 10.470s -> 10.395s, 1.007x speedup, 0.7% improvement, PASS
BS=1: 3177.4ms -> 3167.0ms, 1.00329 speedup, below campaign threshold
BS=8: 5219.3ms -> 5233.4ms, 0.99730 speedup, NOISE verdict
1 lossy shipped op was never accuracy-verified; verified cumulative speedup estimated at 1.0x
Accuracy adjustment applied: raw cumulative 1.053 reduced to verified 1.0, effective shipped = 0
